hook.text: In March 2020, every economic time series snapped. COVID changed the rules overnight.

buildup.text: A structural break is a sudden change in the underlying process generating the data.

discovery.text: After a break, the old trend, seasonality, or variance no longer applies. Models trained on old data fail.

twist.text: Detecting breaks after the fact is easy. Detecting them in real time — as they happen — is extremely hard.

climax.text: The Chow test and CUSUM charts help detect structural breaks, but they need enough post-break data to be sure.

punchline.text: When the rules change, old models break. Detect the shift or be blindsided.

quiz.question: Why are structural breaks problematic for forecasting?
options: They change the underlying process — models trained on old data no longer apply
They always happen gradually enough to detect
